rationale: Postcondition requires r1 == r2 for StrictlyOrderedVec<K>, whose only field is v: Vec<K>. Antecedent gives only r1@ == r2@ == Seq::empty() and validity. Verified via isolated tests that vstd has no Vec view-injectivity axiom: neither v1 == v2 nor v1 =~= v2 nor v1 =~~= v2 is derivable from v1@ == v2@, even with broadcast use of group_vec_axioms. Closing the struct equality would require an assume/admit/external_body axiom, which is forbidden by the task constraints. Inserted view-level equalities (the strongest derivable conclusion) into the proof block as a best-effort artifact.
